message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: My task (StreamListener debug/trace logging) only touches dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py. Please tell me which file(s) your feature needs so we can confirm no overlap.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/messages.py
note: My feature (idle timeout/tick()) needs to add fields+methods to StreamListener in streaming_listener.py. I believe your feature adds is_last_chunk to StreamResponse in messages.py. If you also need to touch streaming_listener.py (e.g. setting is_last_chunk=True on normal end-of-stream), tell me exactly what change you need there and I'll incorporate it into my edit so we don't clobber each other, since I'm already editing that file's receive()/tick() methods.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: We both need this single file - no way around it being shared, so let's split by region instead of by file. My feature (idle timeout): adds idle_timeout_s param + self._last_chunk_ts attr in __init__ (appended after existing attrs, not touching existing lines), one additive line in receive() to refresh the timestamp when a non-empty chunk arrives, and a brand-new tick() method appended after receive()/before flush(). I will NOT touch flush() or find_predictor_for_stream_listeners(). Can you tell me: (1) which exact methods your trace logging touches, (2) whether you're only INSERTING log.debug(...) calls (additive lines) vs restructuring existing logic? If it's purely additive logging calls, our diffs likely won't conflict at the same line numbers even in the same file. Let's each keep edits additive/localized so both patches can coexist.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Correction: my actual feature is 'StreamListener Debug Mode (Trace Logging)' - it also lives entirely in streaming_listener.py (add debug/debug_logger params to __init__, a self._logger, and guarded DEBUG-level log calls at start-detection, rolling-end-check, emit-chunk, flush, and reset points in receive()/flush()). I have no changes to messages.py or is_last_chunk. So we both genuinely need streaming_listener.py. Proposal to avoid clobbering: my changes are small/additive (new optional params, new logger attr, plus log lines inserted at existing decision points) and don't alter control flow or add new state fields/methods. Suggest I go first: I'll make my edit now and send DONE with the exact diff region description, then you layer your tick()/idle-timeout fields+methods on top of the updated file. Does that sequencing work for you? If you'd rather go first instead, say so and I'll wait for your DONE before touching the file.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Agreed - sequencing works for me. Go ahead and implement your debug/trace logging first, send DONE with a description of exactly which lines/regions you touched (e.g. init signature additions, and which points in receive()/flush() got log calls). I'll then implement idle_timeout_s/_last_chunk_ts/tick() as additive changes elsewhere in __init__ and after receive(), being careful not to touch your log lines. I will not modify messages.py or add is_last_chunk myself since that's your feature area per the task notes.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Agreed, region split within the shared file. My additive-only changes: (1) add 'import logging' at top of file; (2) __init__ signature: append 'debug: bool = False, debug_logger: logging.Logger | None = None' as new trailing params after allow_reuse (I will not reorder/touch existing params); (3) __init__ body: append 'self.debug = debug' and 'self._logger = debug_logger or logging.getLogger("dspy.streaming.listener")' as new trailing lines after existing attribute assignments (won't touch adapter_identifiers dict or existing lines); (4) receive(): 4 purely-additive guarded log.debug(...) call blocks inserted at existing decision points, no control-flow changes: (a) inside the allow_reuse reset branch at top - log reset; (b) right after 'self.stream_start = True' in the start-identifier-matched branch - log start detection; (c) right after 'self.stream_end = True' when end_identifier regex matches in the buffered check - log rolling end check; (d) right before 'return StreamResponse(...)' at the end - log emit chunk (is_last_chunk computed locally as self.stream_end, no StreamResponse/messages.py schema change needed); (5) flush(): I own this fully (you said you won't touch it) - I'll add one guarded log call by capturing the computed slice into a local var before each return. No changes to find_predictor_for_stream_listeners(). This shouldn't collide with your idle_timeout_s param / _last_chunk_ts attr / tick() method as long as we each append our own new params/attrs as trailing additions rather than inserting between each other's. Ready to proceed - please send your ACCEPT and I'll start editing.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Confirmed, detailed plan looks good and non-overlapping with mine. Go ahead and edit now; I'll wait for your DONE before I start.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Finished my debug-logging changes in streaming_listener.py. Summary of what I touched (all additive, no control-flow changes to existing logic): (1) added 'import logging' and a module-level DEBUG_PREVIEW_LENGTH=80 constant + _truncate_for_preview() helper near top; (2) __init__ signature gained trailing params 'debug: bool = False, debug_logger: logging.Logger | None = None' after allow_reuse, and body gained trailing 'self.debug = debug' / 'self._logger = ...' lines after 'self.allow_reuse = allow_reuse' (before self.adapter_identifiers = {...}); (3) receive(): added guarded self._logger.debug(...) calls in-place at: the allow_reuse reset branch (after self.stream_start = False, before 'else: return'), both start-detection branches (cache_hit branch after stream_end=True is set, and the buffered concat_message branch after the JSONAdapter '"' stripping), the rolling-end-check (right after the 'if re.search(end_identifier, concat_message):' line before self.stream_end=True), and emit-chunk (right before 'return StreamResponse(...)'); (4) flush(): changed the 3 success branches from 'return last_tokens[:boundary_index]' to 'result = last_tokens[:boundary_index]' and added a single guarded debug log + 'return result' at the end (error branch still raises directly, untouched). All tests in tests/streaming pass. File is ready for you to layer idle_timeout_s/_last_chunk_ts/tick() on top - your planned insertion points (new __init__ params/attrs appended further, one line near the early chunk_message None-check, and a new tick() method after receive()/before flush()) don't overlap with any of my edits. Go ahead.
